title: protium divaricatum subsp. krukoffii
intro: Protium divaricatum subsp. krukoffii, commonly known as the Krukoff's protium, is a plant species native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its bark, which contains a rich source of lupeol, a triterpene with anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Protium divaricatum subsp. krukoffii include its ability to reduce inflammation, alleviate pain, and exhibit antioxidant activity, making it a potential treatment for various health conditions. In herbal preparations, the bark of the Krukoff's protium is often used to make teas, infusions, and extracts, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
